_comment: Stencil mapping for draw.io stencilization. Edit freely; restart of ns_web_start.py is NOT required (mtime-based hot reload). Keys are draw.io official Cisco stencil IDs (mxgraph.*). v2 schema: each shape has 'exact', 'prefix', 'contains' keyword lists. Matching is case-/whitespace-/hyphen-/underscore-/slash-insensitive. Resolution priority for each device: (1) master Default column = 'WayPoint' -> cloud (color-independent), (2) master Stencil Type column starting with 'mxgraph.' -> passthrough, (3) Stencil Type column exact alias match, (4) longest prefix match against device name + non-special attribute columns (Role/Model/OS/Type/...), (5) longest contains match against the same sources, (6) default_shape. Symmetry rule: when Default = 'DEVICE', the 'mxgraph.cisco.storage.cloud' shape is removed from the heuristic candidate set in steps (4) and (5), because a DEVICE row is by definition a real device; cloud rendering for a DEVICE row must be requested explicitly via the Stencil Type column (steps (2)/(3)). Use 'prefix' for short abbreviations (fw, fpr, rt, ap, pc, sw, n9, ir, ncs, hx, ucs) so they only match at the start of a token; use 'contains' for full words (firewall, router, switch) that may appear anywhere. Stencil filter: only cells whose name appears in the master AND whose Default column equals 'DEVICE' or 'WayPoint' are stencilized; color-based filtering has been removed so user palette edits do not affect classification. v1 backward compatibility: a top-level 'aliases' key with flat lists is still accepted and treated as 'exact'-only. Model number expansion convention: 4-digit Cisco model numbers are expanded into four canonical keywords -- the numeric form with the last 2 digits zeroed (granular sub-models are intentionally dropped) plus three textual prefixes 'C##', 'CAT##', 'Catalyst##' built from the first 2 digits. Example: 9166 -> ['9100', 'C91', 'CAT91', 'Catalyst91']. For Cisco Nexus switches an additional 'N##' / 'Nexus##' pair is registered so that DC names like 'Nexus-9332-Spine' or 'N9300-Leaf01' resolve to the Layer 3 stencil. Special note for the 93xx range: Catalyst 9300 (L2 access) and Nexus / generic L3 9300-class both expand to 'C93/CAT93/Catalyst93'; tie-breaking follows JSON declaration order, so workgroup_switch (L2) is intentionally declared BEFORE layer_3_switch in this file -- this aligns with the real-world fact that Catalyst 9300 is most commonly an L2 access switch. Nexus naming (N93/Nexus93/n9 prefix) is unique to L3 and never collides with L2. Override per device with the Stencil Type column when the heuristic generalisation is undesirable (e.g. set Stencil Type = 'mxgraph.cisco.switches.layer_3_switch' for a Catalyst 9300 acting as L3). Keyword sources: classified per Cisco product portfolio data sourced from Catalyst 9000 / Nexus 9000 / Catalyst 9800 / Catalyst 9100 / Cisco 8000 / ISR 1000+4000 / ASR 1000 / Secure Firewall 1000-4200 / UCS C-/B-/X-Series / Catalyst SD-WAN documentation (verified via TechQA-MATCHA Cisco docs corpus, Apr 2026).
